name: asr
description: Asr efficiently copies disk images onto volumes, either directly or via a multicast network stream

subcommands.name: restore
subcommands.description: Restores a disk image or volume to another volume

options.description: Can be a disk image, /dev entry, or volume mountpoint
Can be a /dev entry, or volume mountpoint
When performing a multicast restore, --file can be specified instead of --target
Erases target and is required. --erase must always be used, as file copies are no longer supported by asr
Specifies the destination filesystem format
Suppresses the prompt which usually occurs before target
Specifies num seconds that a multicast client should wait when no payload data has been received over a multicast stream before exiting
Provide progress output that is easy for another program to parse
Skips the verification steps normally taken to ensure that a volume has been properly restored
Allows restores to proceed even if the source's catalog file is fragmented
Forces the restore to use the SHA-256 hash in the image during verification
Tells asr which volume in the source container to invert when doing an APFS restore
Tells asr which volume in the source container to invert when doing an APFS restore
Forces asr to use replication for restoring APFS volumes
Forces asr to use the inverter for restoring APFS volumes
Specifies the snapshot on the source APFS volume to restore to the target APFS volume
Names a snapshot on the source APFS volume to use in combination with --toSnapshot to specify a snapshot delta to restore to the target APFS volume

subcommands.name: restoreexact
subcommands.description: Same as restore verb, except that for an HFS Plus volume, the target partition is resized to match the size of the source partition/ volume

options.description: Can be a disk image, /dev entry, or volume mountpoint

Skips the verification steps normally taken to ensure that a volume has been properly restored

Forces asr to use the inverter for restoring APFS volumes

Source has to be a UDIF disk image
The network interface to be used for multicasting
Server requires a configuration file to be passed

subcommands.name: imagescan
subcommands.description: Calculate checksums of the data in the provided image and store them in the image

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Report the image metadata which was placed in the image by a previous use of the imagescan verb

options.description: Writes its output as an XML-formatted plist
One of the options that control how asr uses memory
